converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Hermon Atkins MacNeil papers
MacNeil, Hermon Atkins, 1866-1947.
2425
.4 cubic feet. .4 cubic feet.
1888-1966. Correspondence, photographs Letters from colleagues, public figures, friends, relatives, and others concerning the execution and sale of various works, his nomination to membership in honorary and professional societies and related professional and social matters; an autobiographical sketch (typescript carbon), 1943, membership certificates and citations, numerous photographs of the artist and his sculpture, newspaper clippings on his career, and miscellaneous printed items. Also, messages of condolence and formal tributes sent to his widow, Cecelia W. Muench MacNeil, obituaries, and press reports concerning a memorial established for the artist. Correspondents include Adriaan Jacob Barnouw, Emile Brunet, Jo Davidson, William Henry Fox, Carl Paul Jennewein, Leon Kroll, and Victor Emanuel III. Collection material in English 2086572
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
March 1, 1976
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, October 2012
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Sculptor.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Letters from colleagues, public figures, friends, relatives, and others concerning the execution and sale of various works, his nomination to membership in honorary and professional societies and related professional and social matters; an autobiographical sketch (typescript carbon), 1943, membership certificates and citations, numerous photographs of the artist and his sculpture, newspaper clippings on his career, and miscellaneous printed items. Also, messages of condolence and formal tributes sent to his widow, Cecelia W. Muench MacNeil, obituaries, and press reports concerning a memorial established for the artist. Correspondents include Adriaan Jacob Barnouw, Emile Brunet, Jo Davidson, William Henry Fox, Carl Paul Jennewein, Leon Kroll, and Victor Emanuel III.
Cite As:
Hermon Atkins MacNeil papers, #2425. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Sculptors. Sculpture. Barnouw, Adriaan Jacob, 1877-1968. Brunet, Emile, 1899- Davidson, Jo, 1883-1951. Emanuel, Victor, III. Fox, William Henry. Jennewein, Carl Paul, 1890- Kroll, Leon, 1884-1974. MacNeil, Cecelia W. Muench.
2086572 <<<<<<< HEAD Link to a printed finding aid.Series I. CorrespondenceBryan Lathrop, Holland House, Kinsley, New York1907-01-091
Scope and Contents
ALS to M. n.p. Lathrop has purchased two of MacNeil's bronzes -Primitive Chant: and Navaho Orator - and wishes to know how many copies are in existence or to be issued.Orson Lowell, College Point, New York.1907-101
Scope and Contents
DS to M., n.p. Handwritten contract wherein Lowell agrees to pay $30 a month for a studio in New York City.Walter L. Palmer, 5 Lafayette St., Albany, N.Y.1909-05-061
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. To M., n.p. Congratulates him for having won the Albany Competition.Karl Bitter (b.1867 - d.1915), University of Michigan, Ann Arbor1909-05-071
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Albany Competition congratulationsAttilio Piccirilli, New York1909-05-071
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Albany Competition congratulationsLorado Taft, 1038 Fine Arts Building, Chicago, Ill.1909-05-071
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Albany competition congratulations; also, thanks him for sending the facts about an exhibition in New York.Leo Lentelli, New York.1909-05-121
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Albany Competition congratulations.Isidore Konti, 154 West 55th Street, New York1909-05-181
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Albany Competition congratulations.Arthur F. Whitin, Whitinsville, Massachusetts.1909-06-021
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Thanks him for sending photographs of the Albany monument; mentions that Professor Hamlin has sailed for England.Charles Mulford Robinson (b.1869 - d.1917) The National Arts Club, Gramercy Park.1909-051
Scope and Contents
ALS M., n.p. Albany Competition congratulationsOrson Lowell, n.p.1910-11-291
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Has had his shop redecorated. Miss McDougall, associate editor of The Designer, visited him. He had just returned from a trip to Chicago.Orson Lowell, n.p.1910-121
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Comments on a bronze bust McNeil sold. Amusing water color sketch at top of sheet.W.H. Holmes, Smithsonian Institute, Washington, D.C.1911-07-131
Scope and Contents
TLS to M., 45 West 22nd Street, New York City. Asks if he would design and execute a gold medal provided for by Alexander Agassiz to be awarded to persons making important deep-sea investigations. 2pcs.William H. Taft, The White House, Washington, D.C.1912-08-171
Scope and Contents
TLS to M., College Point, New York. Asks him to design a gold medal (doesn't say for what in the letter)Edwin Howland Blashfield, (b.1848-d.1925) 48 Central Park South, New York City.1916-03-141
Scope and Contents
ALS to Mr. Breck, n.p. Comments on the difficulty of a problem he had sent him.George Henry Bissell (b.1829 - d.1920) Mount Vernon, New York1916-09-031
Scope and Contents
ALS to Mr. and Mrs. M., n.p. Reminisces about the Latin Quarter in Paris and his days in the army and navy. Refers to himself as Peri Bissell.Paul Wayland Bartlett (b.1850 - d.1925) 239 Randolph Place, N. E., Washington, D.C.1917-12-271
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., College Point, N.Y. Thanks him for a postal card.Daniel Chester French (b.1850 - d.1931), 125 West Eleventh Street, New York City.1919-02-191
Scope and Contents
TLS to M., College Point. Informs him that the Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York City wants to buy a bronze copy of his Sun Vow. Asks if he wants the plaster cast back.Daniel Chester French (b. 1850 - d. 1931), 125 West Eleventh Street, New York City.1919-03-191
Scope and Contents
TLS to M., College Point. The plaster cast will be returned as soon as he wants it.Edwin Howland Blashfield, 48 Central Park South, New York City.1925-03-171
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Suggests that the National Academy of Design try to find a new person to be president instead of himself.Edwin Howland Blashfield, 48 Central Park South, New York City.1925-04-021
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Accepts nomination for the presidency.George Haven Putnam (b.1844 - d.1930), 24 Bedford Street, Strand, W.C. 2, London, England1925-05-271
Scope and Contents
TLS to M., College Point. Discusses the possible nomination of Bertelli to the Century AssociationNicholas Murray Butler, New York.1932-01-261
Scope and Contents
DS to M., n.p. To Hermon A. MacNeil fellow member of the American Academy of Arts and Letters.Robert Underwood Johnson (b.1853 - d.1937, c/o Mr. F.H. Holden, New Milford, Connecticut, R.F.D. 1.1932-08-221
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Discusses the nomination of Stephen Vincent Benet to the Academy of Arts and Letters. Feels some people might vote against him. Walter Johannes Damrosch (b.1862 - d.1950) is also being considered.Robert Underwood Johnson (b.1853 - d. 1937), Hall of fame, New York University , New York City.1932-10-051
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Discusses further the election of people to the Academy - votes required, etc.Anna Huntington, La Pyrole, Leysin, Switzerland.1933-01-061
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Thanks him for a letter congratulating her on her election to the American Academy of Arts and Sciences. Comments on the difficulties of being an artist. (written on the back of a photograph of Alpine and snow scene).Edwin Howland Blashfield, 50 Central Park West, New York City.1933-11-241
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Thank-you note for a visit to MacNeil's home.MacKenzie King, Office of the Prime Minister, Ottawa, Canada1945-09-181
Scope and Contents
TLS to M., College Point, New York. Thanks him for a letter. Hopes he will visit Canada again soon. + envelopeJames Truslow Adams, Sheffield House, Southport, Connecticut.1947-05-121
Scope and Contents
TLS to M., College Point, N.Y. Discusses a museum in South Dakota.George Grey Barnard (b.1863 - d.1938), 700 Fort Washington Avenue, New York Citydate unknown1
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Thanks him for a letter and invites him and his wife to his home.John Burroughs (b.1837 - d. 1921), West Park, N.Y.,date unknown1
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Thanks him for a photo of a bust MacNeil had executed.DS, n.p.1906-02-191
Scope and Contents
A petition signed by MacNeil, other sculptors, and various associates who subscribed money for a fund to send Miss Alice Simpson to Europe.I.a. Letters of Congratulations
Scope and Contents
Correspondence from a scrapbook, compiled by Hermon A. MacNeil's secretary. Scrapbooks dismantled, material placed in bond folders.E. Wallace Mast1946-02-091E. Wallace Mast1946-02-121Marie1946-02-081

Scope and Contents
ALS to M. n.p. Lathrop has purchased two of MacNeil's bronzes -Primitive Chant: and Navaho Orator - and wishes to know how many copies are in existence or to be issued.Orson Lowell, College Point, New York.1907-101
Scope and Contents
DS to M., n.p. Handwritten contract wherein Lowell agrees to pay $30 a month for a studio in New York City.Walter L. Palmer, 5 Lafayette St., Albany, N.Y.1909-05-061
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. To M., n.p. Congratulates him for having won the Albany Competition.Karl Bitter (b.1867 - d.1915), University of Michigan, Ann Arbor1909-05-071

ALS to M., n.p. Albany Competition congratulationsAttilio Piccirilli, New York1909-05-071

ALS to M., n.p. Albany Competition congratulationsLorado Taft, 1038 Fine Arts Building, Chicago, Ill.1909-05-071
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Albany competition congratulations; also, thanks him for sending the facts about an exhibition in New York.Leo Lentelli, New York.1909-05-121
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Albany Competition congratulations.Isidore Konti, 154 West 55th Street, New York1909-05-181

ALS to M., n.p. Albany Competition congratulations.Arthur F. Whitin, Whitinsville, Massachusetts.1909-06-021
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Thanks him for sending photographs of the Albany monument; mentions that Professor Hamlin has sailed for England.Charles Mulford Robinson (b.1869 - d.1917) The National Arts Club, Gramercy Park.1909-051
Scope and Contents
ALS M., n.p. Albany Competition congratulationsOrson Lowell, n.p.1910-11-291
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Has had his shop redecorated. Miss McDougall, associate editor of The Designer, visited him. He had just returned from a trip to Chicago.Orson Lowell, n.p.1910-121
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Comments on a bronze bust McNeil sold. Amusing water color sketch at top of sheet.W.H. Holmes, Smithsonian Institute, Washington, D.C.1911-07-131
Scope and Contents
TLS to M., 45 West 22nd Street, New York City. Asks if he would design and execute a gold medal provided for by Alexander Agassiz to be awarded to persons making important deep-sea investigations. 2pcs.William H. Taft, The White House, Washington, D.C.1912-08-171
Scope and Contents
TLS to M., College Point, New York. Asks him to design a gold medal (doesn't say for what in the letter)Edwin Howland Blashfield, (b.1848-d.1925) 48 Central Park South, New York City.1916-03-141
Scope and Contents
ALS to Mr. Breck, n.p. Comments on the difficulty of a problem he had sent him.George Henry Bissell (b.1829 - d.1920) Mount Vernon, New York1916-09-031
Scope and Contents
ALS to Mr. and Mrs. M., n.p. Reminisces about the Latin Quarter in Paris and his days in the army and navy. Refers to himself as Peri Bissell.Paul Wayland Bartlett (b.1850 - d.1925) 239 Randolph Place, N. E., Washington, D.C.1917-12-271
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., College Point, N.Y. Thanks him for a postal card.Daniel Chester French (b.1850 - d.1931), 125 West Eleventh Street, New York City.1919-02-191
Scope and Contents
TLS to M., College Point. Informs him that the Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York City wants to buy a bronze copy of his Sun Vow. Asks if he wants the plaster cast back.Daniel Chester French (b. 1850 - d. 1931), 125 West Eleventh Street, New York City.1919-03-191
Scope and Contents
TLS to M., College Point. The plaster cast will be returned as soon as he wants it.Edwin Howland Blashfield, 48 Central Park South, New York City.1925-03-171
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Suggests that the National Academy of Design try to find a new person to be president instead of himself.Edwin Howland Blashfield, 48 Central Park South, New York City.1925-04-021
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Accepts nomination for the presidency.George Haven Putnam (b.1844 - d.1930), 24 Bedford Street, Strand, W.C. 2, London, England1925-05-271

TLS to M., College Point. Discusses the possible nomination of Bertelli to the Century AssociationNicholas Murray Butler, New York.1932-01-261
Scope and Contents
DS to M., n.p. To Hermon A. MacNeil fellow member of the American Academy of Arts and Letters.Robert Underwood Johnson (b.1853 - d.1937, c/o Mr. F.H. Holden, New Milford, Connecticut, R.F.D. 1.1932-08-221
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Discusses the nomination of Stephen Vincent Benet to the Academy of Arts and Letters. Feels some people might vote against him. Walter Johannes Damrosch (b.1862 - d.1950) is also being considered.Robert Underwood Johnson (b.1853 - d. 1937), Hall of fame, New York University , New York City.1932-10-051
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Discusses further the election of people to the Academy - votes required, etc.Anna Huntington, La Pyrole, Leysin, Switzerland.1933-01-061
Scope and Contents
ALS to M., n.p. Thanks him for a letter congratulating her on her election to the American Academy of Arts and Sciences. Comments on the difficulties of being an artist. (written on the back of a photograph of Alpine and snow scene).Edwin Howland Blashfield, 50 Central Park West, New York City.1933-11-241

ALS to M., n.p. Thank-you note for a visit to MacNeil's home.MacKenzie King, Office of the Prime Minister, Ottawa, Canada1945-09-181

TLS to M., College Point, New York. Thanks him for a letter. Hopes he will visit Canada again soon. + envelopeJames Truslow Adams, Sheffield House, Southport, Connecticut.1947-05-121

TLS to M., College Point, N.Y. Discusses a museum in South Dakota.George Grey Barnard (b.1863 - d.1938), 700 Fort Washington Avenue, New York Citydate unknown1

ALS to M., n.p. Thanks him for a letter and invites him and his wife to his home.John Burroughs (b.1837 - d. 1921), West Park, N.Y.,date unknown1

ALS to M., n.p. Thanks him for a photo of a bust MacNeil had executed.DS, n.p.1906-02-191
